key_insight: A physics-informed multihead neural network can learn a low-dimensional latent manifold for PDE solution families, revealing robust, reproducible geometry of the solution space beyond the initial condition.
engineering_relevance: This offers a compact surrogate representation for PDE solution ensembles, which could reduce storage, accelerate analysis, and improve interpretability in computational physics workflows.
method: Shared-body PINN learns a latent embedding of the solution family.
Linear heads reconstruct solutions for different initial conditions, with the initial profile built into the output.
A head-orthogonalization penalty removes degeneracies and stabilizes the latent spectrum; Fourier-shell analysis measures frequency contributions to each component.
evidence_signal: On the 1D viscous Burgers equation, latent dimension 20 compressed to about 2-4 principal components for 95% variance and 4-7 for 99%, with similar qualitative behavior on heat and wave equations.
watch_item: The learned latent basis is only identifiable up to a change of basis, so the orthogonalization penalty and frequency analysis must be handled carefully to ensure reproducibility across runs.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Neural Embeddings of PDE Solution Families
